title: Fig. 2 in Species-specific qPCR assays allow for high-resolution population assessment of four species avian schistosome that cause swimmer's itch in recreational lakes
author: Rudko, Sydney P.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Fig. 2. Percent contribution of T. stagnicolae, T. szidati, T. physellae and A. brantae species to each lake. Water samples from different locations and dates were tested using the species-specific qPCR assay and results were pooled by lake to understand the relative contribution overall of each species to each lake. The percent contribution (based on gene copy number) of each species was calculated.
steps_raw: Fig. 2. Percent contribution of T. stagnicolae, T. szidati, T. physellae and A. brantae species to each lake. Water samples from different locations and dates were tested using the species-specific qPCR assay and results were pooled by lake to understand the relative contribution overall of each species to each lake. The percent contribution (based on gene copy number) of each species was calculated.
